mission.title: Install and Configure Armada on Kubernetes
mission.description: Armada is a CNCF sandbox project for multi-cluster batch job scheduling. It enables efficient queuing, scheduling, and management of high-throughput compute workloads across multiple Kubernetes clusters. This mission covers both the recommended Armada Operator method and the manual Helm-based approach.

steps.title: Install the Armada Operator (recommended)
steps.description:
The Armada Operator is the recommended way to install Armada. It uses Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) to manage the lifecycle of Armada services.

```bash
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm repo update
helm install armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system --create-namespace
```

Verify the operator is running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
```

steps.title: Install Armada dependencies
steps.description:
Armada requires several external dependencies:

- **PostgreSQL** — persistent storage for job metadata
- **Redis** — caching layer
- **Apache Pulsar** — message broker for inter-component communication
- **kube-prometheus-stack** — monitoring

Install via Helm:
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo add apache https://pulsar.apache.org/charts
helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

helm install postgresql bitnami/postgresql --namespace armada --create-namespace
helm install redis bitnami/redis --namespace armada
helm install pulsar apache/pulsar --namespace armada
helm install kube-prometheus-stack prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace
```

Wait for all dependencies to be ready:
```bash
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=postgresql --namespace armada --timeout=300s
kubectl wait --for=condition=ready pod -l app.kubernetes.io/name=redis --namespace armada --timeout=300s
```

steps.title: Deploy Armada components via CRDs
steps.description:
With the Armada Operator running and dependencies ready, deploy Armada components using Custom Resources:

```bash
kubectl create namespace armada
kubectl apply -n armada -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/armada-crs.yaml
```

This deploys:
- **Armada Server** — API server for job submission
- **Armada Executor** — runs jobs on worker clusters
- **Armada Scheduler** — schedules jobs across clusters
- **Armada Lookout** — web UI for monitoring jobs (exposed on NodePort 30000)
- **Armada Lookout Ingester** — ingests events for the Lookout UI
- **Armada Event Ingester** — processes job events

Verify all components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

steps.title: Apply default PriorityClass
steps.description:
Armada requires a default PriorityClass for all jobs:

```bash
kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/priority-class.yaml
```

steps.title: Install armadactl CLI
steps.description:
Download the armadactl binary for interacting with the Armada API:

```bash
# Download the latest armadactl for your platform from:
# https://github.com/armadaproject/armada/releases/latest

# Create a config file:
mkdir -p ~/.armada
cat > ~/.armadactl.yaml << EOF
currentContext: main
contexts:
  main:
    armadaUrl: localhost:30002
EOF
```

Test the connection:
```bash
armadactl create queue example
armadactl submit dev/quickstart/example-job.yaml
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that all Armada components are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
```

Access the Lookout UI at `http://localhost:30000` to monitor jobs.

You should see pods for armada-server, armada-executor, armada-scheduler, armada-lookout, and the ingester components.

resolution.summary: A successful installation shows the Armada Operator running in 'armada-system', all Armada components (server, executor, scheduler, lookout, ingesters) running in the 'armada' namespace, and the Lookout UI accessible on port 30000.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add gresearch https://g-research.github.io/charts
helm install armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system --create-namespace

uninstall.title: Remove Armada components
uninstall.description:
Delete the Armada Custom Resources and namespace:
```bash
kubectl delete -n armada -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/armadaproject/armada-operator/main/dev/quickstart/armada-crs.yaml
kubectl delete namespace armada
```

uninstall.title: Remove Armada Operator
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the operator and clean up CRDs:
```bash
helm uninstall armada-operator --namespace armada-system
kubectl delete namespace armada-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove dependencies
uninstall.description:
Uninstall PostgreSQL, Redis, Pulsar, and Prometheus:
```bash
helm uninstall postgresql --namespace armada
helm uninstall redis --namespace armada
helm uninstall pulsar --namespace armada
helm uninstall kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring
kubectl delete namespace monitoring
```

upgrade.title: Update Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the repository to get the latest chart versions:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Armada Operator
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the operator to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade armada-operator gresearch/armada-operator --namespace armada-system
```

The operator will automatically reconcile and update the managed Armada components.

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check pod status after upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada-system
kubectl get pods --namespace armada
```

troubleshooting.title: Operator pods not starting
troubleshooting.description:
Check the operator logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=armada-operator --namespace armada-system
```

Ensure cert-manager is installed if webhooks are enabled:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cert-manager
```

troubleshooting.title: Armada components in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
Usually caused by missing or misconfigured dependencies. Check the logs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada
```

Verify that PostgreSQL, Redis, and Pulsar are running and accessible:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep -E 'postgresql|redis|pulsar'
```

troubleshooting.title: Database connection errors
troubleshooting.description:
Verify PostgreSQL is running and the connection string is correct:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep postgresql
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada | grep -i database
```

troubleshooting.title: Pulsar connection issues
troubleshooting.description:
Verify Pulsar is running and all components are healthy:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep pulsar
kubectl logs -l app=armada-server --namespace armada | grep -i pulsar
```

troubleshooting.title: kube-prometheus-stack not installing
troubleshooting.description:
If you get template errors, upgrade Helm to v3.16.2 or later:
```bash
helm version
```

troubleshooting.title: Lookout UI not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
Verify Lookout is running and the NodePort is exposed:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace armada | grep lookout
kubectl get pods --namespace armada | grep lookout
```

prerequisites.description: Requires a Kubernetes cluster with Helm and kubectl. The Armada Operator manages the lifecycle of Armada components. External dependencies (PostgreSQL, Redis, Apache Pulsar, kube-prometheus-stack) must be installed separately.
